items.title: Bangladesh face bowling conundrum as Australia look to seal series
items.summary: Bangladesh also have questions regarding their middle order as they look to stay alive in the series

items.title: Tigers fall to Australia’s wind ‘trap’ and lacklustre approach
items.summary: Bangladesh pace-bowling coach Talha Jubair admitted that a failure to build middle-order partnerships after quick wickets left them short of a competitive total, resulting in a four-wicket defeat to Australia in the first T20I at the Bir Sreshtho Flight Lieutenant Matiur Rahman Stadium in Chattogram on Wednesday.

items.title: Debutant Davies lives his dream on banner day for Australia's spinners
items.summary: Joel Davies took 3 for 17 as Australia's spinners sank Bangladesh in the first T20I in Chattogram

items.title: Tigresses crumble to heavy loss against Australia
items.summary: A woeful batting display condemned Bangladesh to a crushing nine-wicket defeat against Australia in their second Group A match of the ongoing ICC Women's T20 World Cup at Headingley in Leeds on Wednesday

items.title: Bowlers, Voll power Australia to huge win over Bangladesh
items.summary: Garth, Molineux and Perry shared six wickets between them to restrict Bangladesh to 77 for 8 before Australia raced home with more than 10 overs to spare

items.title: Australia's spinners dominate to hand visitors 1-0 series lead
items.summary: Zampa and Davies, on debut, took three wickets each to bowl Bangladesh out for 131, before Connolly smashed 47 in the chase

items.title: Bangladesh bat against Australia; Litchfield and Gardner out with injuries
items.summary: Phoebe Litchfield has a quad injury, while Gardner has a sprained ankle; Grace Harris and Megan Schutt have replaced them, while Bangladesh have brought in Nahida Akter and Sultana Khatun

items.title: Bangladesh bat with Litton ruled out; Chaudhary and Davies debut for Australia
items.summary: Towhid Hridoy is leading Bangladesh in Litton Das' absence, and they have handed a debut to Abdul Gaffar Saqlain, the quick bowler

items.title: Australia beat Bangladesh by four wickets in 1st T20I
items.summary: Australia eased to a four-wicket victory against Bangladesh in the first of three T20Is in Chattogram on Wednesday.

items.title: Wednesday triple-header: Australia, India look to continue good form; SA, Pakistan eye comebacks
items.summary: Headingley will host Australia vs Bangladesh and India vs Netherlands before South Africa take on Pakistan in Birmingham

items.title: Litton happy to have 'headache of too many' fast-bowling options
items.summary: "It is a healthy competition, and at the same time, how good is that for Bangladesh cricket team?"

items.title: Soumya drafted in as Bangladesh monitor Litton’s fitness
items.summary: Bangladesh are sweating over the fitness of T20 captain Litton Das ahead of the opening match of the three-match T20I series against Australia, which begins tomorrow at the Bir Shrestha Shaheed Flight Lieutenant Matiur Rahman Cricket Stadium in Chattogram.

items.title: Marsh boost for Australia as T20I rebuild begins in Bangladesh
items.summary: Bangladesh have retained the pace attack that caused problems in the ODIs but face a tricky selection call

items.title: Bangladesh quick Hasan delivers ‘world-class’ 9-101 on County debut
items.summary: Hasan is next set to feature against Middlesex at home during the 174th Canterbury Cricket Week from June 19-22.

items.title: Connolly fights off cramps in innings of a lifetime
items.summary: Left hander warded off cramps in Dhaka's searing heat to play one of the great ODI innings and lead Australia to a nervy one-wicket win over Bangladesh

items.title: Ferdous, Sharmin take Bangladesh past Netherlands
items.summary: Netherlands however put up a dazzling display on their T20 World Cup debut to give Bangladesh a scare
